title: Orobanche callieri
taxonomy: Orobanche callieri is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the family Orobanchaceae. This parasitic plant is further classified as a genus within the subfamily Orobanchoideae. Orobanche callieri is a species of broomrape, a group of plants that are known to obtain their nutrients by parasitizing other plants. As a member of the family Orobanchaceae, Orobanche callieri is closely related to other parasitic plants such as Pedicularis and Melampyrum. The classification of Orobanche callieri is also supported by its unique morphology and genetic characteristics.
uses: Orobanche callieri is reported to have potential medicinal properties. However, detailed information on its specific uses and benefits is not readily available. The scientific community continues to explore the potential uses of this plant species.

constituents: Orobanche callieri contains flavonoids, phenylethanoid glycosides, and iridoids as its active constituents. preparations: Orobanche callieri is a parasitic plant, and as such, its use in herbal preparations is not well-documented. intro: Orobanche callieri, commonly known as Callery's broomrape, is a parasitic plant species that belongs to the genus Orobanche, characterized by its yellowish-green flowers and distinctive broom-like habit. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases, such as diabetes, hypertension, and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Orobanche callieri include alkaloids, such as calleryine and calliisine, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Orobanche callieri is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
